summary: Guidance for transforming, filtering, combining, buffering, and timing observable sequences with composable operators.
recommendedPatterns: Prefer operator composition over nested Subscribe trees.
Use timing and combination operators to encode behavior instead of hand-written coordination flags.
Keep pipelines small, named, and observable in tests.
avoidPatterns: Do not reimplement merge, zip, combine, debounce, or buffer behavior imperatively unless necessary.
Do not bury operator chains inside anonymous callbacks that cannot be inspected or tested.
setupSteps: Model the source sequence clearly first.
Apply transformation, filtering, and combination operators in readable stages.
Cover timing-sensitive operators with TestScheduler-based tests.
commonPitfalls: Using the wrong hot/cold assumption when composing operators.
Testing throttle or delay behavior with real time instead of virtual time.

sources.title: Observable Operators (Embedded Offline Catalog)
